pr.title: Use HSD assert macros for hand-expanded asserts
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: PR #2676 was a broad code-quality cleanup, split from #2671, converting hand-expanded __assert and OSReport+__assert patterns to HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, and HSD_ASSERTREPORT where the file argument was __FILE__ or the owning file basename. The PR body reports 102 of 107 sites converted across about 50 files, with 5 non-converted sites left expanded and annotated because macro form did not byte-match or needed structural work. The author reported all 1046 compiled objects remained byte-identical and main.dol sha1 was unchanged.

key_files.role: converted OSReport plus assert to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted repeated item_gobj OSReport plus assert to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted Yoshi error reports plus asserts to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted multiple fighter report/assert sites to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
mixed conversion plus retained non-byte-matching expanded assert with todo
converted plain assert and multiple report/assert sites to HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted device report/assert sites to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
converted repeated dynamics bounds report/assert sites to HSD_ASSERTREPORT
assert macro cleanup plus local rename for macro stringification per PR body
retained expanded assert with todo because macro form did not byte-match inside inline function per PR body
assert macro cleanup plus local rename for macro stringification per PR body
retained expanded assert with todo because structural changes were needed per PR body
retained expanded assert with todo because macro form did not byte-match inside inline function per PR body
assert macro cleanup plus local rename for macro stringification per PR body
retained expanded assert with todo because structural changes were needed per PR body

what_changed: Converted direct __assert calls to HSD_ASSERT when the assertion was plain and the file argument matched __FILE__ or the file's own basename.
Converted direct __assert calls with explicit assertion-message strings to HSD_ASSERTMSG, preserving line numbers and message text.
Converted OSReport followed by __assert patterns to HSD_ASSERTREPORT, preserving report strings and variadic report arguments shown in the diff excerpts.

Renamed selected locals back to original variable names when macro stringification needed to reproduce the original assert string: synth.c ptr/buf to p, and mnmain.c/mndeflicker.c data/menu to user_data, per the PR body.

Left sites unconverted when the macro form did not byte-match or required structural work: ftchangeparam.c float-compare polarity; mnhyaku.c and id.c inside inline functions; tobj.c and plbonuslib.c structural cases. These were annotated with @todo according to the PR body and visible ftchangeparam.c diff excerpt.
Changed 53 files, with 180 added lines and 306 deleted lines according to PR metadata.
smart_moves: Scoped conversion eligibility to cases where the assert file argument was __FILE__ or the owning basename, and explicitly left different-filename cases untouched.
Used HSD_ASSERTREPORT for report-plus-assert blocks rather than dropping OSReport side effects.
Preserved byte identity as the acceptance criterion: the PR body reports all 1046 compiled objects byte-identical and main.dol sha1 unchanged.
Did not force macro cleanup at the five problematic sites; instead retained expanded form and added @todo markers where matching or structure blocked conversion.
Adjusted local variable names only where macro stringification required the original assert text, rather than broadly renaming for style.
decomp_lessons: Assert/report macro cleanup can be source-level cleanup without codegen change when line numbers, file identity, condition text, and report side effects are preserved.
For HSD_ASSERT-style macros, the expression text produced by macro stringification is part of the match surface; local variable names may need to match original assert strings.

Do not blindly convert every __assert expansion: cases with different filename strings, inline-function context, float comparison polarity, or structural requirements may need to stay expanded until a byte-matching form is found.
When replacing OSReport plus __assert, use a report-preserving macro form such as HSD_ASSERTREPORT rather than a plain assert macro.
naming_conventions: Local renames may be justified when the assert macro stringifies the condition and the existing local name would change the emitted assertion string.
Specific PR-supported examples: synth.c ptr/buf were renamed to p; mnmain.c and mndeflicker.c data/menu were renamed to user_data.
No evidence in this PR supports broad semantic renaming beyond assert-string preservation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use HSD_ASSERT(line, condition) for plain owning-file asserts when the macro reproduces byte-identical output.
Use HSD_ASSERTMSG(line, condition, message) when the original expanded assert had an assertion message distinct from the expression text.
Use HSD_ASSERTREPORT(line, condition, report_fmt, ...) when the original pattern included OSReport side effects before asserting.
Keep expanded __assert form and add a targeted @todo when macro conversion fails byte identity or needs structural changes.
Treat filename argument compatibility as a conversion gate: file argument must be __FILE__ or the file's own basename per PR body.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining direct __assert calls with owning-file basename or __FILE__ that may now be convertible to HSD_ASSERT macros.
Search for @todo Convert to @c HSD_ASSERT to revisit non-byte-matching sites from this PR.
Search ftchangeparam.c for scale != 0.0F float-compare polarity before attempting macro conversion.
Search mnhyaku.c and sysdolphin/baselib/id.c for inline-function assert expansions before converting.
Search tobj.c and plbonuslib.c for structural assert cleanup blockers.
Search for OSReport immediately followed by __assert to identify HSD_ASSERTREPORT candidates.

evidence_refs: human_pr_text:PR body conversion rules, exceptions, and verification report

notes: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the loaded PR slice, so review_feedback is empty.
The loaded raw diff is truncated, but changed_files.jsonl provides a complete changed-file list and the PR body states the broad conversion count and exceptions.
Validation results are represented as author-reported PR evidence; this intake did not rerun builds or objdiff.
The PR was merged according to metadata, but no reviewer intent or acceptance rationale beyond merge status is inferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Assert macro cleanup requires preserving file, line, condition text, and report side effects
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2676 shows a broad cleanup where hand-expanded __assert sites were converted to HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, or HSD_ASSERTREPORT only when the file argument was __FILE__ or the owning file basename; OSReport side effects were preserved with HSD_ASSERTREPORT; author-reported verification was byte-identical objects and unchanged main.dol sha1.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:OSReport plus assert converted to HSD_ASSERTREPORT

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep expanded assert form when macro conversion is not byte-matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2676 left five assert sites expanded and annotated with @todo when macro conversion did not produce byte-identical output or needed structural changes, including ftchangeparam.c float-compare polarity, inline-function cases in mnhyaku.c and id.c, and structural cases in tobj.c and plbonuslib.c.

source_update_candidates.title: Mention macro stringification as part of assert macro matching
source_update_candidates.text: When replacing hand-expanded __assert with HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG/HSD_ASSERTREPORT, the macro-stringified condition text can be match-relevant. Local variable names may need to preserve the original assertion string, but such renames should be narrow and evidence-backed.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:synth.c ptr/buf -> p and mnmain.c/mndeflicker.c data/menu -> user_data
source_update_candidates.reason: Current loaded standards cover assert/report macro cleanup generally, but PR #2676 adds concrete evidence that macro stringification can justify narrow local renames.

source_update_candidates.title: Filename compatibility gate for HSD assert macro conversion
source_update_candidates.text: For hand-expanded __assert cleanup, only convert to HSD assert macros when the original filename argument is compatible with the macro expansion, such as __FILE__ or the owning file basename; leave different-filename cases expanded unless another evidence-backed form matches.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:file argument rule and untouched different-filename sites
source_update_candidates.reason: PR body explicitly states the conversion boundary; curator can decide whether this belongs in assert macro standards.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all remaining __assert sites are convertible; the PR explicitly identifies exceptions and filename constraints.
Do not promote the local renames to semantic naming facts for p or user_data beyond assert-string preservation.
Do not claim reviewer-requested changes because the loaded slice contains no review comments or reviews.
